content.explanation:
**Relation & Weather Vocabulary:**

**FAMILY RELATIONS (परिवार):**
| Relation | Hindi |
|----------|-------|
| Grandfather | दादा / नाना |
| Grandmother | दादी / नानी |
| Father-in-law | ससुर |
| Mother-in-law | सास |
| Brother-in-law | साला / देवर / जीजा |
| Sister-in-law | साली / ननद / भाभी |
| Nephew | भतीजा / भांजा |
| Niece | भतीजी / भांजी |
| Cousin | चचेरा/ममेरा भाई/बहन |
| Stepfather | सौतेला पिता |
| Step-mother | सौतेली माँ |

**WEATHER Vocabulary:**
| English | Hindi |
|---------|-------|
| Sunny | धूप वाला |
| Cloudy | बादल वाला |
| Rainy | बारिश वाला |
| Thunderstorm | आंधी-तूफान |
| Foggy | कोहरे वाला |
| Humid | उमस वाला |
| Drizzle | हल्की बारिश |
| Hailstorm | ओले वाली बारिश |
| Breeze | हल्की हवा |
| Cyclone/Typhoon | चक्रवात |
rules: Relations: Use "my + relation": "my cousin", "my niece", "my father-in-law"
Weather: Use "It is + adjective": "It is sunny today."
Weather: Use "There is + noun": "There is fog on the road."
"Humid" vs "wet": humid = umsas (sticky heat); wet = physically wet
Weather phrases: "What is the weather like?" / "How is the weather there?"
In-laws: all husband/wife's family members add "in-law"
content.memoryTrick:
**In-laws = अपने husband/wife के relatives**
Father-in-law = पति/पत्नी का पिता (ससुर)
Sister-in-law = पति/पत्नी की बहन (ननद/साली)

Weather tip: **IT IS + adj / IT IS + Verb+ing**
'It is cold.' / 'It is snowing.'

examples.hindi: मेरे ससुर बहुत अच्छे इंसान हैं।
examples.english: My father-in-law is a very good person.
The weather is very humid today.

examples.hindi: मेरे भांजे की उम्र 5 साल है।
कोहरे की वजह से flight late हुई।
examples.english: The flight was delayed due to fog.
There was a thunderstorm last night.

mistakes.why: "Cousin" itself is neutral — no need to add "brother" or "sister"

mistakes.wrong: It is very hot weather outside.
mistakes.correct: It is very hot outside. / The weather is very hot.
mistakes.why: Not 'hot weather' as object — say 'it is hot' or 'the weather is hot'
'Niece' already means a girl — no need for 'daughter'

speakingTips: Family intro: 'I have a large family — my grandparents, parents, and my nephew all live together.'
Small talk: 'How is the weather there? It is very humid here in Mumbai.'
Describing: 'Yesterday there was a heavy thunderstorm and the power went out.'

vocabulary.example: A blizzard hit the northern states.
India gets heavy rain during monsoon.
Mumbai has high humidity in summer.
She is a descendant of a royal family.
His uncle is his legal guardian.

practice.hindi: वह मेरे पिता के बड़े भाई हैं।
practice.english: He is my father's elder brother.

practice.hindi: वह मेरी माँ की बहन है।
मेरे नाना-नानी गाँव में रहते हैं।
practice.english: My maternal grandparents live in the village.
alternatives: my grandparents from my mother's side live in a village.
my father in law is kind.

practice.hindi: उसके दो बेटे और एक बेटी है।
practice.english: She has two sons and one daughter.
alternatives: she has 2 boys and a girl.
tomorrow is going to be rainy.

practice.english: There are clouds in the sky.

alternatives: the climate is very nice today.
